formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Since the chapter "Magnetism and Matter" does not have a significant number of mathematical formulas, I will provide key statements and laws relevant to the chapter. Here is the JSON array:

[
  {
    "name": "Magnetic Field due to a Current-Carrying Conductor",
    "formula": "B = (μ₀I) / (2πr)",
    "variables": "μ₀: Permeability of free space, I: Current, r: Distance from the conductor",
    "units": "Tesla (T)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the magnetic field at a point due to a current-carrying conductor"
  },
  {
    "name": "Biot-Savart Law",
    "formula": "dB = (μ₀I) / (4π) × (dl × r) / r²",
    "variables": "μ₀: Permeability of free space, I: Current, dl: Element of current-carrying conductor, r: Position vector",
    "units": "Tesla (T)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the magnetic field due to a current-carrying conductor"
  },
  {
    "name": "Magnetic Dipole Moment",
    "formula": "μ = IA",
    "variables": "I: Current, A: Area of the loop",
    "units": "A-m² (Am²)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the magnetic dipole moment of a current-carrying loop"
  },
  {
    "name": "Torque on a Magnetic Dipole",
    "formula": "τ = μ × B",
    "variables": "μ: Magnetic dipole moment, B: Magnetic field",
    "units": "N-m (Newtons-meter)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the torque on a magnetic dipole in a magnetic field"
  },
  {
    "name": "Lorentz Force",
    "formula": "F = q(E + v × B)",
    "variables": "q: Charge, E: Electric field, v: Velocity, B: Magnetic field",
    "units": "N (Newtons)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the force experienced by a charged particle in an electric and magnetic field"
  },
  {
    "name": "Magnetic Moment of an Electron",
    "formula": "μ = -eL/2m",
    "variables": "e: Elementary charge, L: Orbital angular momentum, m: Mass of the electron",
    "units": "A-m² (Am²)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the magnetic moment of an electron"
  }
]

Note: Since there are no significant mathematical formulas in the chapter "Magnetism and Matter", the above list includes key statements and laws relevant to the chapter.
